exercises.title: Complete the Full Diagnostic
exercises.description: Take the comprehensive diagnostic (34 questions, 170 points) from the deep lesson. Score each section honestly. Identify your 2 weakest sections and your 1 strongest section. Write a one-paragraph reflection on what surprised you.
hints: Be honest — this is for your eyes only, so there's no benefit to inflating scores
Low scores aren't failures — they're opportunities for growth
Look for patterns: do wellness scores lag behind productivity scores?

solution.code:
DIAGNOSTIC RESULTS EXAMPLE:

SECTION SCORES:
A. Time Management:     22/30 (Strong)
B. Focus & Deep Work:   18/25 (Good)
C. Goals & Habits:      15/25 (Developing)
D. Stress & Mindfulness: 12/30 (Needs Work)
E. Physical Health:      18/30 (Developing)
F. Work-Life Balance:    16/30 (Needs Work)

TOTAL: 101/170 (Developing)

WEAKEST SECTIONS:
1. Stress & Mindfulness (12/30) — I don't manage stress well
2. Work-Life Balance (16/30) — I check Slack after hours constantly

STRONGEST SECTION:
Time Management (22/30) — I'm good at prioritizing and scheduling

REFLECTION:
'I was surprised that my wellness scores were much lower than my
productivity scores. I've been optimizing for output while ignoring
my health. This explains why I feel exhausted despite being 'productive.'
I need to prioritize stress management and work-life boundaries
before I burn out completely.'

exercises.title: Create Your 12-Week Development Plan
exercises.description: Using your diagnostic results, create a 12-week plan focusing on your 2 weakest areas. Divide into three 4-week blocks, each building one specific habit using the Two-Minute Rule. Define tracking metrics and success criteria for each block.
hints: Start with the area that affects your daily life most
Use the Two-Minute Rule: scale each habit to 2 minutes or less to start
Track completion rate AND outcome — both matter

solution.code:
12-WEEK DEVELOPMENT PLAN:

BLOCK 1 (Weeks 1-4): STRESS MANAGEMENT
Habit: After I close laptop at 5:30 PM, I will do 3 cycles of box breathing
Stack: Attached to shutdown ritual
Tracking: Completion rate, stress level before/after (1-10)
Success criteria: 80% completion, average -2 points stress reduction

BLOCK 2 (Weeks 5-8): WORK-LIFE BOUNDARIES
Habit: After I complete shutdown ritual, I will put work phone in drawer
Stack: Attached to shutdown ritual (builds on Block 1)
Tracking: Completion rate, after-hours checks (target: 0)
Success criteria: 80% completion, no after-hours work for 5+ days/week

BLOCK 3 (Weeks 9-12): MINDFULNESS
Habit: After I pour morning coffee, I will do 3 minutes of breath awareness
Stack: Attached to morning routine
Tracking: Completion rate, focus quality (1-10)
Success criteria: 80% completion, +1 point focus improvement

REASSESSMENT:
Retake the full diagnostic at Week 12
Compare Section D (Stress) and Section F (Work-Life Balance) scores
Target improvement: +8 points each section (16 total)

WEEKLY CHECK-IN TEMPLATE:
| Week | Block 1 | Block 2 | Block 3 | Notes           |
|------|---------|---------|---------|------------------|
| 1    | ✓       | -       | -       | Starting breathing|
| 2    | ✓       | -       | -       | Getting easier   |
| 3    | ✓       | -       | -       | Noticeable calm  |
| 4    | ✓       | -       | -       | Block 1 done!    |
| 5    | ✓       | ✓       | -       | Adding boundary  |
...

exercises.title: Build Your Productivity System Summary
exercises.description: Create a one-page personal reference document summarizing YOUR productivity and wellness system: your time management approach, focus techniques, goal-setting method, stress tools, health routines, and work-life boundaries. This becomes your personal playbook for maintaining your system long-term.
hints: Make it specific to YOU — not generic advice
Include the actual techniques you use, not just theory
Keep it to one page — if it's longer, it won't get used

solution.code:
MY PRODUCTIVITY & WELLNESS PLAYBOOK:

TIME MANAGEMENT:
• Eisenhower Matrix for daily prioritization
• Time block 9-11 AM for deep work (no meetings)
• Batch all meetings into 1-4 PM slot
• Weekly review every Friday at 4 PM

FOCUS:
• Pomodoro 50/10 for coding, 25/5 for reviews
• Slack on Do Not Disturb during deep work
• Phone in another room during focus blocks
• Track pomodoros in Pomofocus.io

GOALS:
• 1-3-5 rule: 1 big, 3 medium, 5 small goals per quarter
• SMART goals for each
• Quarterly OKR review
• Track in Notion

STRESS MANAGEMENT:
• Box breathing: 4-4-4-4 during high-pressure moments
• Body scan at lunch (5 min)
• Shutdown ritual at 5:30 PM daily
• Weekly stress audit (Friday review)

PHYSICAL HEALTH:
• 7-8 hours sleep, consistent 10:30 PM - 6:30 AM
• Morning exercise: 20 min (Mon/Wed/Fri)
• Ergonomic setup: monitor at eye level, hourly posture check
• Water bottle on desk, drink throughout day

WORK-LIFE BOUNDARIES:
• Fixed hours: 9 AM - 5:30 PM
• No Slack/email after shutdown
• Work phone in drawer after hours
• No weekend work unless production emergency

REASSESSMENT:
• Retake diagnostic every 3 months
• Adjust plan based on scores
• One new habit per month maximum

REMEMBER:
• Sleep is the foundation — protect it first
• Consistency > intensity
• Track everything — you can't improve what you don't measure
